page: Microsoft Azure E32as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core and memory performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier, while Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores fall into the strong category. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes remain in the average tier. Cryptographic operations perform well, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the top tier, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing also register in the strong tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32as_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of higher vCPU tiers. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external network storage for data persistence. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise application servers, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong Geekbench and Passmark memory scores. Cryptographic operations are accelerated by built-in instruction sets like AES and SHA_NI. Given its high memory density and strong single-core performance, the E32as_v6 is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-density RAM and top-tier single-core processor performance for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory benchmarks
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or onboard GPUs
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise apps
